external_narratives.summary: American Air Museum archive entry for the Reedham War Memorial, documenting the mid-air collision of two B-17G Flying Fortresses from the 549th and 550th Bomb Squadrons of the 385th Bomb Group on 21 February 1944.
key_details: Part of 900-plane mission returning from Germany

external_narratives.summary: US War Memorials registry entry for the B-17 42-37963 and 42-31370 memorial at Reedham. Documents the memorial stone listing all fallen crew members from the collision.
key_details: Memorial stone lists all 21 crew names

external_narratives.summary: Local newspaper coverage of remembrance events at the Reedham War Memorial. The aircraft were returning from a raid on Diepholz in North West Germany and collided in cloud just minutes from landing at Great Ashfield.
key_details: Lt Warren J. Pease was pilot of 42-37963

external_narratives.summary: Time Team Series 6, Episode 8 documented the archaeological excavation of B-17 42-37963 crash site at Reedham Marshes in 1999. The television excavation brought national attention to the crash site.
key_details: Time Team excavated crash site in 1999
Brought national attention to the memorial

external_narratives.summary: Personnel record for Lieutenant Warren Jay Pease, pilot of B-17G 42-37963 who apparently lost control in cloud and collided with another Fortress in the formation over Reedham Marshes.
key_details: Capt John H. Hutchison in B-17 42-31370 was on his 25th and final mission
